scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B — both PRs alter the `events` table (Alan's migration and Sarah's migration each modify `events`).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. The workspace shows Sarah works Monday/Wednesday/Friday only, so she’s not available Thursday March 13.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — SAML SSO. Josh promised Acme it would follow the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out of this phase (different protocol, ~3 weeks) and the shipped work only included JWTs and OAuth2 for Google/GitHub.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed 4 steps (including "first milestone," which was the prior activation trigger), so "completion" now means a shorter, different set of steps; the metric isn’t comparing the same thing.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. It was created for load testing but “never actually used for load testing” (last deploy Jan 22), so its $500/mo can be reclaimed.

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — each ticket shows "Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25" in the support notes.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: B — The design doc summary lists 2 open questions: pagination (cursor vs offset — Sarah recommends cursor and Alan agrees) and webhook payload format (nested vs flat — still debated in the thread).

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace notes the migration script that maps plan/tier/subscription is src/billing/mapping.rs, which Alan calls "the rosetta stone file."

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. Josh's customer research found all three prospects said CSV is sufficient; no customer requested PDF and Excel came from the sales rep, not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace test stats list "Edge case tests (boundary conditions, race conditions): 0 (0%)" and "End-to-end tests (full API flow): 7 (2%)".

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — Sarah proposed using JWTs configured to feel like API keys: long-lived JWT bearer tokens that preserve JWT benefits (rotation/revocation) while keeping the simple API-key developer experience.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The plan explicitly notes "No delivery confirmation mechanism": a 200 response doesn't guarantee the customer actually processed the webhook, so deliveries could silently fail and only surface later when invoices don't reconcile.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B) Takeshi at NovaTech in Tokyo — the doc says he offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective and hasn't been asked yet (and he responds quickly).

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace lists ~1h (hardcoded URL) + 45m (retry) + 1h (generic errors) = ~2.75 hours/week; the skipped test causes missed bugs but no added weekly time estimate.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked. Sarah already built a webhook signature module, so adding Ed25519 is localized to that module and should take only about half a day.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break-even at feature 5. Solve 5N = 15 + 2N → 3N = 15 → N = 5 (matches the workspace note: break-even at feature 5; net positive from feature 6 onward).

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace investigation says this is a spec ambiguity: "subscription start date" was interpreted as billing-period start (what engineering built) while Acme expects the original signup date. It's not a backend or data bug; the fix is a 1‑line UI change (rename label or show both dates).

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Adding annual billing expands billing logic (proration/refunds/dunning) and would bite into the already-tight Billing Migration work scheduled for the March 31 launch (no buffer; reconciliation and webhook work are already blocking).

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring caught the issue in 12 minutes, and the root cause was a process gap: the migration wasn't tested against production‑scale data with full historical records (legacy accounts missing plan_change_date).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — Combined baseline is 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec (95% of the 100 req/sec limit). With 1.5–2x spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec, which exceeds the 100 limit and will cause 429 errors.
